question: How do Worker Threads differ from child_process.fork?

options.text: Workers run in the same process, fork creates a separate one
Workers can share memory via SharedArrayBuffer
Workers consume less memory (~5-10 MB vs ~30 MB)
Workers are safer — a crash doesn't affect the process
options.explanation: The opposite: a worker crash in the same process can damage the entire process. fork is more isolated

options.text: Workers automatically use IPC for communication
options.explanation: Workers use postMessage (structured clone), not IPC. IPC is a fork mechanism

options.text: ArrayBuffer is transferred — becomes unavailable in the sender
ArrayBuffer is copied — both threads get their own copy
options.explanation: Without transferList data is copied. With transferList it's transferred — that's the whole point

options.text: Error — ArrayBuffer cannot be passed to Worker
options.explanation: ArrayBuffer can be both copied and transferred

options.text: ArrayBuffer is shared between threads like SharedArrayBuffer
options.explanation: Transfer moves data, not shares it. Use SharedArrayBuffer for sharing

question: Why can't Atomics.wait be called on the main thread?

options.text: It blocks the event loop, making the application unresponsive
The main thread doesn't have access to SharedArrayBuffer
options.explanation: The main thread can work with SharedArrayBuffer. The limitation is only on blocking wait

options.text: Atomics only work in Worker threads
options.explanation: Most Atomics operations work in any thread. Only wait is blocking
The issue isn't deadlock but event loop blocking. Use Atomics.waitAsync for non-blocking waiting

question: What is the optimal Worker Pool size?

options.explanation: For CPU-bound tasks, more threads than cores provides no benefit — threads compete for CPU
Optimal count depends on core count. On a 16-core server, 4 threads underutilize resources

options.text: 1 thread per task in the queue
options.explanation: Creating a thread costs ~5-30ms and ~5-10 MB RAM. With 1000 tasks that's 10 GB RAM
